originalAlarm.description: `eckey` could be null (null value originating from line 10) and is dereferenced.

model:
(define-fun SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 35ae2e259e4.c is memory leak (PRODUCT_30: TRUE, unix.Malloc). This alarm detects a null dereference at L17: eckey = malloc() at L10 can return NULL, and when rand()%2==0 (else branch), *eckey = 15 at L17 dereferences it. L17 is inside #else of #if defined(SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto) (L14-18), so it is only reached when \!SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto. The PC {HAVE_ECC=T, SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto=F} exactly captures when this null dereference occurs — HAVE_ECC=T is required for the eckey code to compile, and \!SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto is required for L17 to be reached. Correctly constrained. New bug.
